summary: North Atlantic signals favored Frederiksen: Nathanielsen said IA 'obviously leans red' and struggled to see a blue constellation. Skaale pointed to Frederiksen. Enhedslisten's Dragsted signaled tactical softening — no ultimatums, everyone needs to bend.

developments.description: Nathanielsen publicly leaned red at Marienborg: 'Vi hælder selvfølgelig rødt' and said she struggled to see a viable blue constellation. Tied participation to Greenlandic foreign-policy demands.
developments.impact: GL-IA pRed up sharply, pFlexible and pBlue down

developments.description: Skaale reported pointing to Frederiksen on arrival at Marienborg. Coordinated Greenlandic posture continued.
developments.impact: GL-NAL pRed up moderately via coordinated posture

developments.description: Dragsted said EL enters negotiations without ultimatums and accepts all sides need to bend. Wealth-tax demand maintained but tactical posture softened.

parameterChanges.justification: 'Vi hælder selvfølgelig rødt' + 'svært at se' blue constellation (Herald/Ritzau, 31/3)
Foreign-policy conditionality retains meaningful flexibility but less than before
Nathanielsen explicitly struggled to see blue constellation
Indirect red shift via coordinated posture and Nathanielsen's gravity; no direct Qarsoq quote
Small blue reduction consistent with red-leaning Greenlandic coordination
Dragsted: 'no ultimatums, everyone needs to bend, we're prepared to do that' (The Local/Ritzau, 1/4)

confidenceAssessment: medium-high — direct quotes from actors at Marienborg; Dragsted quote is first-person and explicit
